wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the NexaMed project overview section. Explain the purpose of this medical clinic management system, its target audience (private medical practices, clinics), and core value proposition. Document the system architecture overview, highlighting the React + TypeScript + Vite stack with modern UI components. Include the key features such as dashboard analytics, patient management, appointment scheduling, consultation tracking, order management, and configuration systems. Provide context about the medical-themed design approach and how it digitizes traditional healthcare workflows. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and explain the relationship between different system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the NexaMed routing system. Detail the React Router configuration, route definitions, and nested routing patterns used for navigation between clinic management sections. Document the routing hierarchy, protected routes, and navigation flow between pages like Dashboard, Patients, Consultations, Agenda, and Orders. Explain the route-based rendering strategy, parameter handling, and navigation state management. Include examples of programmatic navigation, route guards, and dynamic route generation. Address the integration with the layout system and how routes map to page components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dashboard Analytics feature. Explain the analytics overview system, statistics cards display, upcoming appointments widget, recent patient activity feed, and alert system functionality. Document the real-time data visualization components, key performance indicators, and clinic metrics presentation. Include details on dashboard customization options, data refresh mechanisms, and integration with backend analytics services. Provide examples of how administrators can interpret dashboard insights for clinic management decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Button Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Button component. Document all button variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link), sizes (sm, md, lg, icon), and states (disabled, loading). Explain the component's props interface, styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes, and integration with Radix UI. Include usage examples showing different button types, custom styling options, and accessibility attributes. Document the component's role in the overall UI system and best practices for button placement and labeling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for NexaMed development setup. Include prerequisites (Node.js, npm/yarn), step-by-step installation instructions, environment configuration, and first run guidance. Document the development server setup with Vite, TypeScript compilation, and Tailwind CSS configuration. Explain the project structure briefly and how to navigate between different sections. Include common development commands, hot reload functionality, and basic troubleshooting for initial setup. Provide guidance on browser compatibility and recommended development tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the NexaMed layout system architecture. Explain the component hierarchy with Layout as the main wrapper, Sidebar for navigation, and Header for dynamic content display. Document the responsive design implementation, collapsible sidebar functionality, and mobile-first approach. Detail the layout composition pattern, prop drilling strategies, and state management for layout components. Include the integration with routing system, navigation state synchronization, and layout customization options. Address the Tailwind CSS styling approach and responsive breakpoints used throughout the layout system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Patient Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Patient Management system. Cover comprehensive patient database operations including patient registration, medical history tracking, allergy management, contact information maintenance, and demographic filtering. Document the patient search and filtering functionality, medical record organization, and data privacy considerations. Include form validation patterns, patient data lifecycle management, and integration with appointment scheduling. Explain the relationship between patients and their medical consultations, orders, and billing records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Card Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Card component system. Document the Card container, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, and CardFooter components. Explain the component composition pattern, prop interfaces, and styling approach. Include usage examples showing different card layouts, content organization patterns, and responsive design considerations. Document the component's role in displaying structured information and best practices for card hierarchies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the NexaMed application structure. Describe the component hierarchy, layout system design with nested routing, and the provider pattern used for navigation context. Document the React Router configuration, route-based navigation between clinic management sections, and the shared layout wrapper approach. Explain the separation of concerns between pages, components, and utilities. Include the component composition patterns, prop drilling strategies, and state management approaches. Address the responsive design implementation and mobile-first approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Hierarchy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the NexaMed component hierarchy and architectural patterns. Explain the separation between page-level components and reusable UI components, documenting how App.tsx serves as the central orchestrator. Detail the component composition patterns, prop passing strategies, and state management approaches across different component levels. Document the relationship between pages (Dashboard, Patients, Consultations, etc.) and their corresponding layout wrappers. Include the component lifecycle management, performance considerations, and best practices for component organization. Address the TypeScript interface usage and type safety throughout the component hierarchy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Appointment Scheduling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Appointment Scheduling system. Document the interactive calendar interface, appointment status management, availability scheduling, and reminder system functionality. Explain the calendar view modes, drag-and-drop scheduling, conflict detection, and resource allocation. Cover appointment types, duration management, provider availability, and patient capacity limits. Include integration with patient management, consultation tracking, and notification systems. Document the approval workflow and administrative controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Input component. Document input variants, sizes, disabled states, and validation feedback patterns. Explain the component's integration with form handling, error states, and accessibility features. Include usage examples showing different input types, validation scenarios, and styling customization. Document best practices for input field design, user experience patterns, and responsive behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for NexaMed's core features and functionality. Provide an overview of all major modules including Dashboard analytics, Patient Management, Appointment Scheduling, Medical Consultation tracking, Order Management, Configuration settings, and Authentication system. Explain the purpose and scope of each feature area, their interconnections, and how they contribute to the overall medical clinic workflow. Document the user roles and permissions, common workflows, and integration patterns between different features. Include screenshots or wireframe descriptions where appropriate.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for state management patterns in NexaMed. Detail the React hooks implementation for local component state, form state management, and data fetching strategies. Document the state synchronization between components, prop drilling patterns, and context usage where applicable. Explain the state management for different features like patient data, appointment scheduling, and consultation tracking. Include examples of controlled components, form validation states, and loading/error state handling. Address performance optimization techniques, state normalization, and memory management considerations in the React component architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Medical Consultation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Medical Consultation tracking system. Document consultation documentation workflows, vital signs recording, diagnostic planning, prescription generation, and clinical note management. Explain the consultation lifecycle from scheduling to completion, template-based documentation, and integration with patient medical histories. Cover the relationship between consultations and orders, laboratory results, and follow-up appointments. Include privacy considerations, audit trails, and regulatory compliance aspects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Badge Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Badge component. Document badge variants (secondary, destructive, outline), sizes, and styling approaches. Explain the component's role in displaying status information, labels, and metadata. Include usage examples showing different badge types, color schemes, and positioning patterns. Document accessibility considerations and best practices for badge usage in medical contexts.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the NexaMed UI component library. Document each primitive component including Button variants and sizes, Input fields with validation, Card components, Badge system, Dropdown menus, Tabs navigation, Avatar components, Label elements, and Separator utilities. Explain the component APIs, prop interfaces, styling approaches, and customization options. Include usage examples with code snippets, accessibility considerations, and responsive design patterns. Document the integration with Radix UI primitives and Tailwind CSS theming system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Order Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Order Management system. Document laboratory order creation, imaging order management, interconsultation requests, and order status tracking functionality. Explain the order workflow from creation to completion, integration with external services, and result reporting. Cover order categorization, priority management, and automated notifications. Include the relationship between orders and patient consultations, billing integration, and report generation. Document approval workflows and administrative oversight capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dropdown Menu Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dropdown Menu component system. Document the DropdownMenu provider, trigger, content, item, separator, and sub-menu components. Explain the component's integration with Radix UI, keyboard navigation, and accessibility features. Include usage examples showing different menu configurations, nested menus, and interaction patterns. Document best practices for menu organization and user experience design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for NexaMed's TypeScript interfaces and type definitions. Detail the core entity models including User, Consultorio, Paciente, Consulta, OrdenMedica, Cita, and Archivo structures. Document field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints. Explain the type safety implementation, form validation patterns, and data transformation utilities. Include examples of data structures, common operations, and integration with UI components. Address type guards, optional properties, and polymorphic data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Configuration and Settings system. Document system settings management, user account configuration, practice information setup, subscription management, and administrative controls. Explain role-based access control, permission management, and security configurations. Cover practice branding, operational settings, and integration preferences. Include backup and restore procedures, system maintenance options, and troubleshooting configuration issues. Document the relationship between configuration changes and system behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tabs Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Tabs component system. Document the Tabs provider, list, trigger, and content components. Explain the component's state management, keyboard navigation, and accessibility features. Include usage examples showing different tab arrangements, dynamic content loading, and responsive behavior. Document best practices for tab organization and user interface patterns in medical applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for NexaMed's styling and theming system. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration, custom medical-themed color palette, animation system, and responsive design patterns. Explain the utility class usage, component styling approaches, and theme customization options. Include guidelines for maintaining consistency across components, accessibility considerations for color schemes, and best practices for extending the design system. Document the CSS architecture, global styles, and component-specific styling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Authentication and Security system. Document the login interface, session management, user authentication flow, and access control mechanisms. Explain password policies, multi-factor authentication options, and security best practices. Cover user role management, permission inheritance, and audit logging. Include session timeout handling, secure token management, and integration with healthcare compliance requirements. Document the relationship between authentication and protected route access throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Avatar Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Avatar component. Document avatar variants, sizes, image handling, and fallback mechanisms. Explain the component's integration with user profiles, image loading, and accessibility features. Include usage examples showing different avatar configurations, placeholder handling, and responsive behavior. Document best practices for avatar usage in medical applications and patient information displays.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for NexaMed's build system and development workflow. Document the Vite configuration, TypeScript compilation settings, PostCSS processing, and development server setup. Explain the build optimization strategies, production bundling, and asset handling. Include development best practices, debugging techniques, and performance optimization approaches. Document the dependency management, script commands, and deployment preparation processes. Address common build issues and troubleshooting strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Label Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Label component. Document label variants, styling approaches, and integration with form elements. Explain the component's role in form accessibility, user guidance, and error communication. Include usage examples showing different label configurations, form field associations, and responsive behavior. Document best practices for label design and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Separator Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Separator component. Document separator variants (horizontal, vertical), styling approaches, and spacing considerations. Explain the component's role in layout organization, content separation, and visual hierarchy. Include usage examples showing different separator configurations, responsive behavior, and integration with other layout components. Document best practices for separator usage in medical interfaces.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# NexaMed - Medical Clinic Management System

## Project Introduction

NexaMed is a comprehensive medical clinic management system designed to streamline healthcare operations for private medical practices and clinics. The application provides a modern, responsive interface for managing patient records, appointments, medical consultations, and administrative tasks.

### Purpose Statement
The system aims to digitize traditional medical clinic workflows, reducing paperwork while improving patient care coordination and administrative efficiency.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Digital Patient Management**: Centralized patient records with comprehensive health history tracking
- **Appointment Scheduling**: Integrated calendar system for managing patient appointments
- **Clinical Documentation**: Structured medical consultation recording and reporting
- **Administrative Efficiency**: Streamlined billing, inventory, and staff management
- **Data Analytics**: Real-time insights into clinic performance and patient trends

### Target Audience
- Private medical practitioners and clinics
- Healthcare professionals seeking digital transformation
- Medical practice administrators and receptionists

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern React-based frontend architecture with a clean separation of concerns and reusable component design patterns.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[React Application] --> B[Routing System]
    B --> C[Layout Components]
    C --> D[Page Components]
    D --> E[UI Components]
    E --> F[Utility Functions]
    
    G[TypeScript Types] --> A
    H[State Management] --> A
    I[API Integration] --> A
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style C fill:#f3e5f5
    style E fill:#e8f5e8
```

### Component Breakdown

**Application Structure**
- **Entry Point**: Vite-powered React application with TypeScript support
- **Routing**: React Router DOM for navigation between different sections
- **Layout System**: Modular layout components with sidebar navigation
- **Component Library**: Reusable UI components built with Radix UI primitives
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom medical-themed color palette

**Design Patterns**
- **Component Composition**: Reusable UI components with consistent APIs
- **State Management**: React hooks for local component state
- **Type Safety**: Comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for data structures
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with adaptive layouts

### System Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Router] --> B[Layout Wrapper]
    B --> C[Sidebar Navigation]
    B --> D[Main Content Area]
    D --> E[Dashboard]
    D --> F[Patient Management]
    D --> G[Appointment Scheduling]
    D --> H[Medical Records]
    D --> I[Configuration]
    
    E --> J[Statistics Cards]
    E --> K[Recent Activity]
    F --> L[Patient List]
    F --> M[Search & Filters]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Root** (`src/App.tsx`)
- Defines the complete routing structure for the application
- Implements nested routes with shared layout components
- Manages navigation between different clinic management sections

**Build Configuration** (`vite.config.ts`)
- Vite build system with React plugin
- Path alias configuration for clean imports
- Development server setup with hot module replacement

### Core Modules

**Layout System** (`src/components/layout/`)
- **Layout Component**: Main wrapper with collapsible sidebar and header
- **Sidebar Navigation**: Persistent navigation with route-based menu items
- **Header Component**: Dynamic title and description display

**UI Component Library** (`src/components/ui/`)
- **Button System**: Variants for different actions (default, destructive, outline)
- **Form Components**: Input fields, labels, and validation helpers
- **Display Components**: Cards, badges, separators for structured content
- **Navigation Components**: Dropdown menus, tabs for complex interactions

**Utility Functions** (`src/lib/utils.ts`)
- **CSS Class Management**: Enhanced clsx/tailwind-merge combination
- **Date Formatting**: Spanish locale date and datetime formatting
- **Age Calculation**: Automatic age computation from birth dates
- **ID Generation**: Random ID creation for temporary entities

### Configuration Approach

**Tailwind CSS Configuration** (`tailwind.config.ts`)
- Custom medical-themed color palette (medical-50 to medical-950)
- Animation system for smooth transitions and fade effects
- Responsive breakpoints optimized for medical interface needs
- Dark mode support with configurable themes

**TypeScript Configuration** (`tsconfig.json`)
- Strict type checking with modern JavaScript features
- Path mapping for clean import statements
- JSX configuration for React development

### External Dependencies

**Runtime Dependencies** (`package.json`)
- **React Ecosystem**: Core React library with React DOM for rendering
- **Routing**: React Router DOM for client-side navigation
- **UI Primitives**: Radix UI components for accessible interfaces
- **Icons**: Lucide React for consistent iconography
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS utilities with merge functionality
- **Charts**: Recharts for medical data visualization
- **Date Handling**: date-fns for robust date manipulation

**Development Dependencies**
- **Build Tools**: Vite for fast development and production builds
- **TypeScript**: Type checking and compilation
- **Linting**: ESLint with React hooks and refresh plugins
- **PostCSS**: CSS processing with autoprefixer and Tailwind integration

### Integration Points

**Component Relationship Architecture**

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[App Component] --> B[Layout Component]
    B --> C[Sidebar Component]
    B --> D[Header Component]
    B --> E[Outlet Content]
    
    E --> F[Dashboard Page]
    E --> G[Pacientes Page]
    E --> H[Consultas Page]
    E --> I[Ordenes Page]
    E --> J[Agenda Page]
    E --> K[Configuracion Page]
    
    F --> L[Statistics Cards]
    F --> M[Citas Today]
    F --> N[Recent Patients]
    
    G --> O[Patient List]
    G --> P[Search & Filters]
    G --> Q[Patient Details]
    
    style A fill:#e3f2fd
    style B fill:#f1f8e9
    style F fill:#fff3e0
    style G fill:#f3e5f5
```

**Feature Architecture Flow**

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Login
    Login --> Dashboard: Authentication Success
    Dashboard --> Pacientes: Navigate to Patients
    Dashboard --> Consultas: Navigate to Consultations
    Dashboard --> Agenda: Navigate to Schedule
    Dashboard --> Ordenes: Navigate to Orders
    Dashboard --> Configuracion: Navigate to Settings
    
    Pacientes --> PatientDetails: Select Patient
    PatientDetails --> Consultas: Add Consultation
    PatientDetails --> Agenda: Schedule Appointment
    
    Consultas --> ConsultaForm: New Consultation
    ConsultaForm --> Dashboard: Save & Return
    
    Agenda --> CitaDetails: Select Appointment
    CitaDetails --> Dashboard: Update Status
    
    Configuracion --> Dashboard: Save Settings
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Dashboard Analytics**
- Real-time statistics cards showing clinic metrics
- Upcoming appointments display with status indicators
- Recent patient activity timeline
- Alert system for pending tasks and reminders

**Patient Management System**
- Comprehensive patient database with searchable records
- Detailed medical history tracking
- Allergy and medication management
- Contact information and emergency contacts
- Age calculation and demographic filtering

**Appointment Scheduling**
- Interactive calendar interface
- Appointment status tracking (scheduled, attended, cancelled)
- Patient availability management
- Reminder system for upcoming visits

**Medical Consultation Tracking**
- Structured consultation documentation
- Vital signs monitoring
- Diagnostic and treatment planning
- Prescription and order generation

### Implementation Highlights

**Responsive Design System** (`src/components/ui/`)
- Consistent component API across all UI elements
- Theme-aware styling with medical color palette
- Accessible form controls with proper labeling
- Mobile-optimized touch targets and interactions

**Data Visualization** (`src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`)
- Statistical cards with trend indicators
- Interactive charts for patient demographics
- Performance metrics with comparative analytics
- Export capabilities for medical reports

**State Management Pattern**
- Local state management for UI interactions
- Form state handling with validation
- Search and filter functionality
- Modal dialogs for detailed views

**Performance Optimization**
- Lazy loading for page components
- Efficient rendering with React.memo patterns
- Optimized bundle size through tree shaking
- Fast refresh during development

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](NexaMed-Frontend/package.json)
- [App.tsx](NexaMed-Frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [Layout.tsx](NexaMed-Frontend/src/components/layout/Layout.tsx)
- [Dashboard.tsx](NexaMed-Frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx)
- [vite.config.ts](NexaMed-Frontend/vite.config.ts)
- [tailwind.config.ts](NexaMed-Frontend/tailwind.config.ts)
- [utils.ts](NexaMed-Frontend/src/lib/utils.ts)
- [button.tsx](NexaMed-Frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx)
- [Pacientes.tsx](NexaMed-Frontend/src/pages/Pacientes.tsx)
- [index.ts](NexaMed-Frontend/src/types/index.ts)

</blog>
